query: How frequently does the CDC issue new Travel Health Notices for North American countries (Mexico, Canada, Caribbean, Central America)? List recent CDC travel health notices issued in 2025 and 2026 for North American countries with their issue dates. What is the typical monthly rate of new notices affecting North America?
answer:
I've completed the research on CDC Travel Health Notices for North American countries. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Frequency of CDC Travel Health Notices for North America

**Issuance cadence:** The CDC issues THNs on a **rolling, event-driven basis** (outbreaks, sporadic cases, events, disasters), not a fixed schedule. Globally, CDC posts/updates **several notices per month (often 3–8)**, but only a small fraction touch North American countries.

**North America-specific rate:** Roughly **4–6 new/updated notices per year**, or **~0.3–0.6 per month**. Africa dominates the overall global count (e.g., a March 2026 batch covered 32+ countries, almost all Sub-Saharan Africa).

## Recent notices affecting North America (2025–2026)

| Notice | Level | Country | Date |
|---|---|---|---|
| Diphtheria in Haiti | 1 | Haiti | Jun 25, 2026 (New) |
| Hepatitis A in Canada | 1 | Canada (Manitoba) | Jun 4, 2026 (New) |
| Global Dengue (update) | 1 | Mexico + Caribbean/Central America | Jun 23, 2026 |
| RMSF in Mexico | 1 | Mexico (border states) | Dec 23, 2025 (re-issued) |
| Oropouche in the Americas | 1 | Cuba, Panama | Jan 31 → Sep 18, 2025 |
| Global Measles | 1 | Worldwide | May 28, 2025 |
| Salmonella Newport in Mexico | 1 | Mexico | May 30, 2024 (still active) |
| New World Screwworm (HAN/guidance) | — | 8 countries incl. Mexico | 2025–Jan 2026 |

**Key patterns:**
- Most North American notices are **Level 1** (lowest severity)
- Many arrive via **regional/global umbrella notices** (Oropouche, Global Dengue, Global Measles) rather than dedicated country notices
- **Mexico** is the most frequently named; **Canada** is rare (the June 2026 Hepatitis A notice was notable)
- **Vector-borne/food-borne diseases** dominate

Data is point-in-time (live CDC page accessed July 10, 2026); CDC removes notices as outbreaks resolve.
